name: Teatime Shortbread
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 325°F (160°C).
2. Using an electric mixer, beat the butter and sugar in a bowl until well combined.
3. Sift the flour and salt into the butter mixture.
4. Using fingertips, rub the mixture until moist clumps form.
5. Gently knead the dough until it forms a soft texture.
6. Divide the dough into two equal pieces.
7. Press one piece of dough to form a 9.5-inch round, about 1/4 inch thick.
8. Roll the dough lightly with a rolling pin to smooth the top.
9. Using a 3-inch diameter cookie cutter, cut out 8 cookies. Reserve scraps.
10. Repeat pressing, rolling, and cutting with the remaining dough piece.
11. Gather the dough scraps together.
12. Press the scraps to form another round, then cut out additional cookies.
13. Arrange the cookies on two large baking sheets.
14. Pierce each cookie several times with a fork.
15. Bake until pale golden, about 12 minutes.
16. Cool slightly.
17. Transfer cookies to racks and cool completely.
